prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
(record_tamper_evidence_case mfg_site_primary)
(assign_response_team_to_case mfg_site_primary response_team_alpha)
(associate_forensic_sample_with_case mfg_site_primary forensic_sample_1)
(validate_case mfg_site_primary forensic_sample_1)
(flag_production_line_for_investigation mfg_site_primary production_line_1 forensic_sample_1)
(assign_batch_to_secure_hold_location mfg_site_primary secure_hold_warehouse_1)
(engage_site_mitigation mfg_site_primary production_line_1 secure_hold_warehouse_1)
(release_secure_hold_location mfg_site_primary secure_hold_warehouse_1)
(release_forensic_sample mfg_site_primary forensic_sample_1)
(record_tamper_evidence_case dist_partner_primary)
(assign_response_team_to_case dist_partner_primary response_team_bravo)
(associate_forensic_sample_with_case dist_partner_primary forensic_sample_1)
(validate_case dist_partner_primary forensic_sample_1)
(flag_distribution_center_for_investigation dist_partner_primary distribution_center_east forensic_sample_1)
(assign_batch_to_secure_hold_location dist_partner_primary secure_hold_warehouse_1)
(engage_partner_mitigation dist_partner_primary distribution_center_east secure_hold_warehouse_1)
(release_secure_hold_location dist_partner_primary secure_hold_warehouse_1)
(release_forensic_sample dist_partner_primary forensic_sample_1)
(form_recall_operation_standard mfg_site_primary dist_partner_primary production_line_1 distribution_center_east recall_operation_primary)
(associate_forensic_sample_with_case mfg_site_primary forensic_sample_1)
(mark_recall_for_report_ingestion recall_operation_primary mfg_site_primary forensic_sample_1)
(mark_site_for_execution mfg_site_primary recall_operation_primary)
(mark_partner_for_execution dist_partner_primary recall_operation_primary)
(release_forensic_sample mfg_site_primary forensic_sample_1)
(record_tamper_evidence_case incident_coordination_center_a)
(assign_response_team_to_case incident_coordination_center_a response_team_charlie)
(associate_forensic_sample_with_case incident_coordination_center_a forensic_sample_1)
(validate_case incident_coordination_center_a forensic_sample_1)
(ingest_laboratory_report incident_coordination_center_a lab_report_1 recall_operation_primary)
(authorize_coordinating_unit_phase_one incident_coordination_center_a lab_report_1 recall_operation_primary forensic_sample_1)
(assign_logistics_provider_to_unit incident_coordination_center_a logistics_provider_1)
(assign_batch_to_secure_hold_location incident_coordination_center_a secure_hold_warehouse_1)
(confirm_logistics_provider_assignment_primary incident_coordination_center_a logistics_provider_1 secure_hold_warehouse_1 lab_report_1 recall_operation_primary)
(assign_regulatory_liaison_to_unit incident_coordination_center_a regulatory_liaison_primary)
(authorize_unit_execution_stage_1 incident_coordination_center_a regulatory_liaison_primary lab_report_1 recall_operation_primary)
(finalize_unit_authorization incident_coordination_center_a)
(assign_notification_template_to_case incident_coordination_center_a notification_template_primary forensic_sample_1)
(authorize_unit_and_allocate_team incident_coordination_center_a response_team_charlie notification_template_primary)
(release_logistics_provider_from_unit incident_coordination_center_a logistics_provider_1)
(release_secure_hold_location incident_coordination_center_a secure_hold_warehouse_1)
(release_regulatory_liaison_from_unit incident_coordination_center_a regulatory_liaison_primary)
(release_forensic_sample incident_coordination_center_a forensic_sample_1)
(associate_forensic_sample_with_case mfg_site_primary forensic_sample_1)
(assign_notification_template_to_case mfg_site_primary notification_template_primary forensic_sample_1)
(authorize_manufacturing_site_and_allocate_team mfg_site_primary response_team_alpha notification_template_primary)
(release_forensic_sample mfg_site_primary forensic_sample_1)
(record_tamper_evidence_case incident_coordination_center_b)
(assign_response_team_to_case incident_coordination_center_b response_team_alpha)
(associate_forensic_sample_with_case incident_coordination_center_b forensic_sample_1)
(validate_case incident_coordination_center_b forensic_sample_1)
(ingest_laboratory_report incident_coordination_center_b lab_report_2 recall_operation_primary)
(authorize_coordinating_unit_phase_one incident_coordination_center_b lab_report_2 recall_operation_primary forensic_sample_1)
(assign_logistics_provider_to_unit incident_coordination_center_b logistics_provider_1)
(assign_batch_to_secure_hold_location incident_coordination_center_b secure_hold_warehouse_1)
(confirm_logistics_provider_assignment_primary incident_coordination_center_b logistics_provider_1 secure_hold_warehouse_1 lab_report_2 recall_operation_primary)
(assign_regulatory_liaison_to_unit incident_coordination_center_b regulatory_liaison_primary)
(authorize_unit_execution_stage_1 incident_coordination_center_b regulatory_liaison_primary lab_report_2 recall_operation_primary)
(finalize_unit_authorization incident_coordination_center_b)
(assign_notification_template_to_case incident_coordination_center_b notification_template_primary forensic_sample_1)
(authorize_unit_and_allocate_team incident_coordination_center_b response_team_alpha notification_template_primary)
(release_forensic_sample incident_coordination_center_b forensic_sample_1)
(associate_forensic_sample_with_case dist_partner_primary forensic_sample_1)
(assign_notification_template_to_case dist_partner_primary notification_template_primary forensic_sample_1)
(authorize_distribution_partner_and_allocate_team dist_partner_primary response_team_bravo notification_template_primary)
message.reasoning:
The problem is a planning problem in PDDL for a tamper evidence breach response domain. I need to find a plan that achieves the goals: execution_authorized for mfg_site_primary, dist_partner_primary, incident_coordination_center_a, and incident_coordination_center_b.


## Understanding the Problem  
- The task involves creating a plan in PDDL for a tamper evidence breach response domain.  
- The goal is to ensure execution_authorized status for mfg_site_primary, dist_partner_primary, incident_coordination_center_a, and incident_coordination_center_b.

## Examining Entity Relationships  
- Production_line_1 is linked to mfg_site_primary, and distribution_center_east connects to dist_partner_primary.  
- Incident coordination centers a and b, assigned to the site and partner, handle recall responsibilities and generate reports.  

## Analyzing Entity Types  
- Manufacturing_site, distribution_partner, and incident_coordinating_unit are all classified as case_entities in the domain hierarchy.  
- These entities stem from case_entity_root and fit into the case_entity_group structure, confirming their relevance to the problem.  

## Exploring Authorization Steps  
- To achieve execution_authorized, actions like authorize_manufacturing_site_and_allocate_team and authorize_unit_and_allocate_team are needed, requiring notification_committed and other preconditions.  
- Notification_committed requires operation_flagged_for_execution, which can be set by actions like mark_site_for_execution or finalize_unit_authorization, depending on the entity.  

## Investigating Case Opening  
- Cases can be opened using record_tamper_evidence_case for each case_entity, with preconditions like not case_opened and not execution_authorized.  
- Only one forensic_sample_1 is available, and associating it consumes it, potentially limiting paths for some entities.

## Analyzing Execution Paths  
- To flag operations for sites and partners, actions like mark_site_for_execution require the entity to be ready, mitigations engaged, and recall processes activated.  
- For units, finalize_unit_authorization or finalize_pha_action_and_flag_execution can set operation_flagged_for_execution, each with specific preconditions like unit_execution_ready.  

## Exploring PHA Process  
- Engaging public health authority requires a validated unit, sample association, and available PHA contact, consuming PHA availability.  
- Finalizing PHA actions sets unit_execution_confirmed and flags execution, but execution_authorized needs further steps like notification commitment.  

## Checking Validation Requirements  
- Validation is needed for PHA engagement, requiring the case to be opened, team assigned, and sample associated with the unit.  
- Regulatory liaison assignment, also part of the process, consumes available liaisons, with options to release them if needed.  

## Investigating Notification Steps  
- Notification_committed, required for authorization, needs the operation flagged, sample associated, and a notification template available.  
- Authorizing units involves allocating teams and templates after notification commitment, ensuring all preconditions like case opening are met.

## Assessing PHA Engagement  
- Engaging public health authority (PHA) requires a validated unit, sample association, and available PHA contact, with only one PHA contact (local) initially available for incident_coordination_center_a.  
- PHA engagement consumes the available PHA contact, and no release action exists, limiting engagement to one unit (e.g., center_a), as center_b's regional PHA contact is not initially available.  

## Evaluating Unit Authorization Paths  
- Units can be flagged for execution via finalize_unit_authorization, requiring unit_execution_ready, which involves confirming logistics provider assignment (primary or secondary) after phase one.  
- Phase one authorization (authorize_coordinating_unit_phase_one or advance_unit_regulatory_acknowledgement) needs a validated unit, report ingestion, and sample association, with options depending on logistics and regulatory requirements.  

## Exploring Resource Constraints  
- Only one forensic sample and one PHA contact are available, potentially bottlenecking case openings and PHA engagements for multiple entities (e.g., centers a and b).  
- Team availability (three teams for four entities) suggests a sequential approach: open and process three cases, authorize one to free a team, then handle the fourth.

## Examining Execution Readiness  
- Making both site and partner execution-ready is necessary for recalling, requiring actions like engage_site_mitigation or allocate_mitigation_inventory_to_site.  
- Allocate_mitigation_inventory_to_site is simpler, consuming mitigation stock and setting the site ready without needing sample association, but doesn't engage mitigation.  

## Analyzing Recall Formation  
- Forming a standard recall requires site and partner mitigation engaged, line assigned, and center associated, which allocate_mitigation doesn't fully support.  
- For recalls with logistics requirements, allocating for the site (no mitigation engaged) and engaging the partner (with sample association) can work, depending on center inventory status.  

## Reviewing Partner Actions  
- Engaging a partner for mitigation requires flagging the distribution center under investigation, needing validated partner and sample association.  
- Allocating to a partner sets it ready without mitigation engaged, affecting recall formation options based on regulatory or logistics needs.

## Reviewing Partner Actions  
- After engaging a partner, the hold can be released using release_secure_hold_location, making it available for other actions without affecting quarantine status.  
- Forming a recall operation requires both site and partner to be mitigation engaged, which can be achieved by sequencing actions like open, assign team, associate sample, validate, flag, assign hold, engage mitigation, and release hold.  

## Exploring Sample Management  
- Forensic samples need to be associated and released strategically; after marking a recall, samples can be reassociated for further actions like marking for report ingestion.  
- Releasing a sample from one entity (e.g., site) allows associating it with another (e.g., partner) for actions like assigning notification templates, ensuring compliance with recall formation steps.  

## Analyzing Notification Commitment  
- Assigning a notification template to a case requires the entity to be flagged, sample associated, and template available, consuming the template upon commitment.  
- Authorization (e.g., authorize_manufacturing_site_and_allocate_team) can then proceed, freeing the team and making the template available again for subsequent actions.

## Sequencing Recall Execution  
- Authorizing unit execution stage 1 requires phase two, assigned regulatory liaison, and lab reports, setting the unit execution ready without final checks for standard recalls.  
- Finalizing unit authorization flags the unit as confirmed after ensuring execution readiness, maintaining sample association throughout the process.  

## Managing Resource Allocation  
- Assigning notification templates to cases consumes templates and commits them, freeing teams and templates for reuse after authorization.  
- Logistics and regulatory liaisons remain assigned post-authorization, requiring release for reuse in subsequent units, such as center_b, if resources are limited.  

## Exploring Phase Transitions  
- Confirming logistics primary advances the recall to phase two, even for standard recalls without logistics requirements, relying on phase one and quarantine status.  
- Sample association persists through phase transitions, supporting actions like notification template assignment without needing re-association.

## Exploring Resource Release  
- Logistics, regulatory liaison, and hold can be released after finalizing unit authorization, ensuring they’re available for subsequent units like unit_b.  
- Releasing these resources after confirming phase two is possible, but confirm_logistics_primary and quarantine status require them to remain assigned initially.  

## Managing Team Allocation  
- Teams like charlie, assigned to unit_a, are released after authorization, freeing them for use in unit_b, while site and partner teams (alpha, bravo) remain committed until their authorization.  
- Reusing teams requires sequencing site and partner authorization first to free alpha and bravo, enabling efficient team allocation for units.  

## Sequencing Unit Operations  
- Units depend on recall actions and ready reports, which require marking recalls after forming them, necessitating site and partner readiness first.  
- To validate units, samples must be associated and released from sites, ensuring proper sequencing to avoid conflicts in team and sample allocation.

## Releasing Unit Resources  
- Resources like logistics providers, secure hold locations, and regulatory liaisons are released after unit authorization, making them available for reuse in unit_b.  
- Forensic samples are also released post-authorization, ensuring no conflicts in subsequent operations like unit validation.  

## Managing Team Availability  
- Authorizing the site frees alpha team, allowing its reassignment to unit_b, while bravo remains committed to the partner.  
- Notification templates and forensic samples are re-associated for site authorization, ensuring smooth transitions and resource availability.  

## Executing Unit_b Operations  
- Unit_b follows a similar process to unit_a, using lab_report_2 and team alpha for validation and ingestion.  
- Logistics and regulatory assignments for unit_b are confirmed, finalizing authorization and freeing resources like alpha for future use.  

## Finalizing Partner Authorization  
- After unit_b, forensic samples are released and re-associated with the partner for template assignment and authorization.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
